description: Upstream-written message keys not covered elsewhere. 'error' in both wild shapes typed by ResponseMessage.svelte:90 (boolean | {content}): a0 carries the legacy boolean form, a2 the {'content': ...} object the backend persists on provider failure (middleware.py:3450-3460) and the frontend stores on chat:message:error (Chat.svelte:538-539). a1 carries 'annotation' (rating/reason/comment/tags saved by ResponseMessage.svelte:456-475 and persisted by the subsequent updateChatById), 'originalContent' written by the outlet-filter path alongside the rewritten content (middleware.py:3408), and 'merged' — the multi-model merge response object set by mergeResponses (Chat.svelte:2746-2752, message.merged = mergedResponse). a3 carries 'selectedModelId' + 'arena': true as written by the frontend for arena models (Chat.svelte:1852-1853); the backend also writes selectedModelId (middleware.py:3466-3471).

e0e0e0e0-1111-4222-8333-444455556666.content: Compare the tradeoffs of SQLite WAL mode for mobile apps.

a1b1c1d1-1111-4111-8111-111111111111.content:
WAL mode gives concurrent readers during writes and fewer fsyncs, at the cost of extra -wal/-shm files and checkpointing you must manage.

-- footer appended by outlet filter --
a1b1c1d1-1111-4111-8111-111111111111.originalContent: WAL mode gives concurrent readers during writes and fewer fsyncs, at the cost of extra -wal/-shm files and checkpointing you must manage.

merged.content: Both models agree: WAL trades extra sidecar files and checkpoint management for concurrent reads and better write throughput on mobile.

e1e1e1e1-3333-4333-8333-777788889999.content: Now answer the same question with a random arena model.

a3b3c3d3-4444-4444-8444-333333333333.content: WAL is usually the right default on mobile: readers never block on the writer, and small transactions amortize well across checkpoints.

messages.content: Compare the tradeoffs of SQLite WAL mode for mobile apps.

WAL mode gives concurrent readers during writes and fewer fsyncs, at the cost of extra -wal/-shm files and checkpointing you must manage.
